mnemonics: Parallel I/O: Don't Be A SLOB (Simultaneous, Locking, Overhead, Bandwidth)
keyPoints: Parallel I/O requires careful coordination to prevent data corruption from simultaneous access.
Techniques like file striping across multiple disks are used to increase I/O bandwidth.
Collective I/O operations (like MPI-IO) combine small requests into large ones to improve efficiency.
commonMistakes: Assuming parallel file access is automatically faster than serial access without proper design.
Not properly handling file locks, leading to race conditions and corrupted data.
Ignoring the network and disk hardware limitations when designing a parallel I/O strategy.
